objective: Test whether a target-adaptive external trust gate can independently certify controlled false-accept routing on multiple fresh protein-complex targets.

never_supported_by_this_protocol: one universal pAE threshold transfers to every target
recertification of any previously evaluated panel

ordered_modes.condition: fit empirical false-accept rate is at most target_alpha
fit has both classes, AUROC(-pAE_interaction to success) is at least 0.65, and a frozen threshold accepts at least minimum_fit_accepts with empirical false-accept rate at most target_alpha

certification_rule.certified_when: the frozen fit-stage rule accepts at least 22 certification rows and its one-sided exact upper confidence bound is at most 0.2

completed_unlock_conditions: exact one-sided binomial bound is implemented and unit-tested in trust-core and designer compatibility code
complex gate and panel reporting can consume an explicitly selected exact bound without fallback to Hoeffding
the W2b evaluator enforces fit-mode freezing, exact certification, reporting-only test data, and disjoint stage candidate IDs
eight label-blind fresh fit targets pass historical target/source exclusion, source diversity, eight-of-eight sequence clustering, and schema preflight
remaining_unlock_conditions: all eight target MSAs and MSA reports are generated, synced, and pass strict manifest checks
fit, certification, and test seed namespaces are enforced as disjoint
a dry-run packet binds the manifest SHA and this protocol SHA
explicit operator approval is recorded after all prior conditions pass
post_hoc_changes_forbidden: dropping failed or refused initial targets from the multiplicity denominator
using the test split to choose a mode or threshold
